name: Current Council Proposal
description: Ballot revenue measure plus the budget reduction plan already under discussion: vacancy freeze, targeted cuts, and reserve bridges.

name: If Revenue Fails — Cuts Only
description: Maximum feasible spending cuts and reserve draws with no new revenue. Painful and still leaves a gap.

name: Keep Everything Running
description: Raise revenue rather than cut services. Asks residents to pay more.

name: Protect Core Services
description: Preserve public safety and essential services. Cut discretionary programs.

name: Efficiency First
description: Make city government leaner through staffing discipline and outsourcing.

name: Structural Reform
description: Redesign how services are delivered. Slower but more sustainable.

name: Spend Less Overall
description: Broad and deep spending cuts. Services will be reduced.

name: Use Savings to Get Through
description: Bridge the gap with reserves and deferrals. Buys time but doesn't fix anything.

name: Balanced Approach
description: Small tax increase, targeted cuts, and one structural change. Share the pain.

name: Status Quo (Current Approach)
description: The approach Berkeley currently uses — bridges gaps with one-time funds without fixing the underlying structural problem.

name: Structural Balance
description: Closes the gap with lasting changes to revenues and spending. No temporary measures.

name: Delay the Problem
description: Maximum short-term relief, maximum future pain. Every lever here makes next year harder.

name: Close the Gap
description: Revenue increases plus targeted spending cuts — sized to fully close the structural deficit.

name: Protect Services
description: Preserve programs. Fund the gap with taxes and efficiency — no outsourcing, no elimination.

name: Shift Delivery Model
description: Change how services are provided — contracting, concessions, and county transfers.
